name: Junyu Jiang
bio: Junyu Jiang's research examines the interaction between media and politics, with a focus on how political concepts are contested and reshaped, particularly in a constrained media environment. He uses network analysis and computational text analysis to map the dynamic discourse of identity politics. His work investigates how the concept of the "nation" is negotiated in electoral discourses, and how digital infrapolitics, ranging from Han ethno-nationalism to pseudo-sacred illusions, shape public opinion on social media platforms. Currently, he works at the Ukrainian Poetry Digital Archive, investigating the patterns of pronoun usage in wartime. Currently a master's student in Computational Social Science at UC San Diego, he holds a B.A. (Honors) from the Communication University of China, completed through a joint program with the University of Missouri-Columbia. He is currently applying for the Ph.D programme in 26 fall.
